test.name: Write a proved-tier proof for the completed father-identification research
test.description: The flynn-research-complete-no-proof scenario has all planned searches completed including a negative probate result. The skill should produce a `proved` tier proof — research is now reasonably exhaustive (per the populated stop_criteria in q_001) and four independent original sources converge on the conclusion. The 1845 Irish Catholic baptism provides primary information on the father-child relationship; the 1860 census provides corroborating indirect evidence (household position, no explicit relationship column until 1880). Tests the high-confidence tier path, distinct from the `probable` tier the seed test covers.

input.user_message: Write the final proof conclusion for Patrick Flynn's parentage.

judge_context: Should justify the proved tier by explaining why the search is reasonably exhaustive (q_001's `exhaustive_declaration` is populated with stop_criteria, all planned searches including the negative probate are complete) and why three independent original sources converge on the conclusion
Should incorporate the negative probate result (log_006) into the narrative — exhaustive search includes documenting what was searched and didn't turn up, not just what did
Should cite the resolved birthplace conflict c_001 within the narrative — proof conclusions don't omit resolved conflicts; they're part of the GPS audit trail showing the analytical work

`narrative_markdown` should be a standalone, FamilySearch-uploadable proof document with inline citations to all four sources (src_001 through src_004) — a reader with no access to research.json should be able to follow it
